interpretation: Availability pressure captures how constrained tracked GPU capacity looks.
Price pressure captures whether capacity is becoming expensive.
Frontier pressure focuses on high-demand accelerator classes.
Provider depth reduces false conviction from single-provider observations.
Latest RPCT captures current resource pressure.
Scarcity ties forecast risk back to observed GPU market pressure.
Shock delta measures abrupt movement versus recent baseline.
Depth measures how much provider evidence is present.
Availability score measures observed capacity depth.
History score rewards repeat observations over time.
Dislocation measures cross-provider price dispersion for comparable GPUs.
Composite stress blends scarcity, forecast, dislocation and reliability pressure.
Performance tracks whether the signal system is becoming more trustworthy over time.
